title: Smart apparel
abstract: A garment for use in system for monitoring the technique of a user undertaking a physical activity which comprises a securing region for securing the garment against a part of the users body, wherein said securing region comprises an interface connector suitable for connecting to a processing unit, and said garment further comprising at least one data transmission path connected to said interface connector and suitable for connection to a sensor and/or a control switch In use the garment 100 enables the collection of data associated with the motion of the user, the comparison of this data with a biomechanical model of the exercise activity and the provision of a feedback response to the user. This enables the user to enhance their technique, and therefore their performance and reduce the likelihood of injuries.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A garment for use in a system for monitoring the technique of a user undertaking a physical activity, the garment comprising a securing region for securing the garment against a part of the users body, wherein said securing region comprises an interface connector suitable for connecting to a processing unit, and said garment further comprising at least one data transmission path connected to said interface connector and suitable for connection to a sensor and/or a control switch. 2. A garment according to claim 1 wherein said garment further comprises at least one sensor for detecting at least one parameter relating to the motion of the user, said sensor connected to said interface connector by at least one data transmission path. 3. A garment according to claim 2 wherein said sensor is located within said securing region. 4. A garment according to any of claims 1 to 3 in which the garment additionally comprises means for providing a feedback response to the user. 5. A garment according to claim 4 in which the means for providing a feedback response to the user is at least one actuator embedded in the garment. 6. A garment according to claim 5 wherein the at least one actuator is one or more of haptic actuator, thermal actuator, peltier tiles, TENS actuator, or electro-active polymer or micro-piezo actuator. 7. A garment according to claim 5 or 6 wherein the at least one actuator is located in said securing region. 8. A garment according to any of claims 5 to 7 wherein said actuator is connected to said interface connector via at least one data transmission path. 9. A garment according to any of claims 1 to 8 further comprising a control switch connected to said interface connector via at least one data transmission path. 10. A garment according to any of claims 1 to 9 wherein said securing region is a waistband of the garment. 11. A garment according to any of claims 1-10 in which at least one data transmission path is embedded within the garment. 12. A securing region for attachment to a garment that may be used in physical activity, wherein said securing region secures the garment against a part of the users body and comprises an interface connector suitable for connecting to a processing unit, and said securing region further comprising at least one data transmission path connected to said interface connector and suitable for connection to a sensor and/or a control switch. 13. A system for monitoring the technique of a user undertaking a physical activity, the system comprising: at least one garment worn by the user, the garment incorporating or carrying at least one sensor for the detection of at least one parameter relating to the motion of the user; a processing unit configured to receive information about the at least one parameter from the at least one sensor, to compare the or each parameter with at least one aspect of a biomechanical model of the physical activity, and to determine if a feedback response is required; and means embedded in or carried by the garment for providing the feedback response to the user during the physical activity. 14. A system according to claim 13 wherein the biomechanical model comprises a database of kinematic parameters and optimal ranges for each of the parameters, and wherein the model is generated by an analysis of the motion of a plurality of individuals. 15. A system according to claim 14 wherein the plurality of individuals comprises a cohort of high performance individuals and a cohort of low performance individuals. 16. A system according to any one of claims 13 to 15 in which the physical activity is running. 17. A system according to any one of claims 13 to 16 in which the means for providing a feedback response to the user is at least one actuator. 18. A system according to claim 17 in which the at least one actuator is embedded in at least one garment. 19. A system according to claim 17 or claim 18 wherein the at least one actuator is one or more of haptic actuator, thermal actuator, peltier tiles, TENS actuator, electro-active polymer or micro-piezo actuator. 20. A system according to any of claims 13 to 19 wherein the garment is a garment as claimed in any of claims 1 to 11. 21. A method for monitoring the technique of a user undertaking an physical activity, the method comprising the steps of: (i) determining at least one parameter relating to the motion of the user with a sensor incorporated or carried within a garment worn by the user; (ii) comparing the or each parameter with at least one aspect of a biomechanical model of the physical activity to determine whether a feedback response is required; (iii) providing the feedback response to the user during the physical activity, wherein said means for providing feedback is embedded in or carried by said garment. 22. A method according to claim 21 in which the feedback response is provided by at least one actuator embedded in or carried by the garment. 23. A method according to claim 22 wherein the at least one actuator is one or more of a haptic actuator, thermal actuator, peltier tiles, TENS actuator, electro-active polymer or micro-piezo actuator. 24. A method according to any one of claims 21 to 23 wherein the biomechanical model is generated by an analysis of the motion of a plurality of individuals. 25. A method according to claim 24 wherein the plurality of individuals comprises a cohort of high performance individuals and a cohort of low performance individuals. 26. A method according to any one of claims 21 to 25 wherein the garment is a garment as claimed in any of claims 1 to 11.
